channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Says Iran War Could Last Weeks and Gives Competing Visions of New Regime
item.description: In a brief interview, he said the country’s hardened military should simply surrender their weapons to the Iranian public.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump boarding Air Force One at Palm Beach International Airport in West Palm Beach, Fla., on Sunday. He spoke to The Times in a brief phone interview.

item.title: How the Assault on Iran Unfolded
item.description: Despite warnings after an earlier wave of killings, top Iranian officials gathered in person, and Israel seized the chance to kill Iran’s supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally to mourn the supreme leader, Ayatollah Khamenei in Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: Oil Prices Jump After Iran Attack
item.description: How long prices remain high will depend on what the United States, Israel and Iran do next.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
item.media:description: An oil tanker anchored near Oman was ablaze on Sunday in this photo taken from a video.

item.title: Epstein’s New Mexico Ranch Gets Scrutiny at Last. It May Be Too Late.
item.description: Heinous allegations have prompted state officials to restart an inquiry, which went cold in 2019, into the convicted sex offender’s lesser-known property.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault and Paul Ratje

item.title: Why the Epstein Investigations Took So Long and Did So Little
item.description: Decades of tips and investigations yielded charges against only two people. A combination of missed chances, narrow laws and prosecutors’ limited focus helps explain why.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Weiser, Matthew Goldstein and Mike Baker
item.media:description: An undated photo of Jeffrey Epstein released by the Justice Department.

item.title: How Talks Between Anthropic and the Defense Dept. Fell Apart
item.description: The Pentagon and Anthropic were close to agreeing on the use of artificial intelligence. But strong personalities, mutual dislike and a rival company unraveled a deal.
item.dc:creator: Sheera Frenkel, Cade Metz and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: In January, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth published a memo calling on A.I. to be widely integrated across the military and for A.I. companies to offer their technology without restrictions.

item.title: The Texas Senate Primary Offers a Test Case for Each Party
item.description: Should Democrats concentrate on swing voters or their base? Can more traditional Republicans win in the MAGA era? Tuesday’s Senate primary in Texas will show the direction the parties are taking.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer and J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Lexi Parra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator John Cornyn of Texas, a four-term Republican Senator, is spending record sums to try to fend off a primary challenge from Ken Paxton, the state’s attorney general, who is beloved by the MAGA base.

item.title: Screen Actors Guild’s Actor Awards Winners List: Updating Live
item.description: The vampire drama was named outstanding cast and, in an upset, its star won best lead actor. Other victors included Catherine O’Hara for “The Studio.”
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan
item.media:description: A surprised Michael B. Jordan accepting the award for “Sinners.”

item.title: Unforgettable SAG Actor Awards 2026 Looks: Teyana Taylor, Emma Stone and More
item.description: Painted torsos, tulip skirts, Jazz Age style and more.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk
item.media:description: Emma Stone sported a pastel sweater with a spaghetti-strap dress to match.

item.title: China’s Parents Are Outsourcing the Homework Grind to A.I.
item.description: Parents in China are turning to A.I. chatbots and other tools to help their children gain an edge and ease the fighting over homework.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang and Jiawei Wang

item.title: Bagram Air Base Targeted by Pakistan, Afghanistan Says
item.description: The Afghan military said it had thwarted a Pakistani airstrike on the former U.S. base, Bagram airfield, amid an intensifying campaign that has targeted dozens of military sites across the country.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Safiullah Padshah
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder for The New York Times
item.media:description: Taliban fighters at the Bagram air field in Afghanistan in 2021.

item.title: Kickstarter’s C.E.O. on Running a Fully Remote Company With a Four-Day Workweek
item.description: Everette Taylor took over Kickstarter at a low moment for the crowdfunding pioneer, which made its name with small projects but now attracts major multinationals and celebrities.
item.dc:creator: Jordyn Holman
item.media:credit: George Etheredge for The New York Times
item.media:description: Everette Taylor has changed the business model of the crowdfunding platform into what he calls Kickstarter 2.0.

item.title: Bring On Defunct: The iPod Enthralls Young Music Listeners
item.description: It’s not exactly going analog, but people looking to move away from streaming say Apple’s old device is a small step away from constantly being online.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang

item.title: In Alysa Liu and Eileen Gu, China and America See a Mirror Image
item.description: The Olympic athletes are the subject of uncomfortable public comparisons that present online narratives that overlap more than both sides may realize.
item.dc:creator: Li Yuan
item.media:description: Ms. Liu, during a training session at the Milan ice skating arena.

item.title: Banks Are Becoming Bulwarks for Vulnerable Seniors
item.description: Older Americans are losing billions of dollars annually to financial exploitation. Banks and investment firms are training employees to spot red flags and stop the transactions.
item.dc:creator: Paula Span

item.title: 4 Ways to Enjoy Your Savings in Retirement Without Going Broke
item.description: Once paychecks stop, it can be frightening to shift out of the savings habit. But there are strategies to enjoy your nest egg and make it last.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Rabasca Roepe
item.media:credit: Amber Baesler for The New York Times
item.media:description: LeeAnn and Randy Smith had spent decades saving and investing, but were afraid they’d run out of money and have to rely on their children.

item.title: ‘Scream 7’s’ Matthew Lillard is a Fan Favorite. If Only Hollywood Understood.
item.description: After a period of joblessness when he had to sell his home and cars, the “Scream” actor’s career is booming — whether Quentin Tarantino likes it or not.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Spencer
item.media:credit: Adali Schell for The New York Times
item.media:description: “I am the best kept secret in Hollywood because the reality is, I go to conventions and my lines are crazy long,” Lillard said.

item.title: Friedrich Merz Has Bigger Problems Than Trump
item.description: Friedrich Merz’s chancellorship is in choppy waters.
item.dc:creator: Anna Sauerbrey
item.media:credit: Jan Staiger for The New York Times

item.title: Images the Iranian Regime Doesn’t Want You to See
item.description: These X-rays show evidence of the Iranian regime’s massacre.
item.dc:creator: Fatemeh Jamalpour, Roxana Saberi, Francesca Trianni and James Robinson

item.title: F.B.I. Investigating if Shooting at a Bar in Austin Has Terrorism Connection
item.description: The shooter killed two and injured 14 early Sunday at an Austin bar. The police and the F.B.I. searched the home of a suspect who was shot and killed by authorities at the scene.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra, Sonia A. Rao, Chelsia Rose Marcius and Ramón Ramirez
item.media:credit: Ricardo B. Brazziell/Austin American-Statesman, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Three people, including the shooter, were pronounced dead at the scene of the shooting on Sunday in Austin, Texas, the police said.

item.title: The American Comedian Who Became a Funnyman in China
item.description: Jesse Appell left everything behind to pursue a comedy career in China, where Western-style club comedy was just finding its footing.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb
item.media:credit: Alex Welsh for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jesse Appell turned his fascination with Chinese culture into a decade-long comedy career there. He tells the story in a new memoir, “This Was Funnier in China: An American Comedian’s Cross-Cultural Journey.”

item.title: With a New Nigeria Refinery, Africa’s Richest Man Wants ‘to Rescue the Country’
item.description: Aliko Dangote, Africa’s richest man, has an ambitious vision for the continent’s most populous nation, but hurdles stand in his way.
item.dc:creator: Dionne Searcey
item.media:credit: Victor Adewale for The New York Times
item.media:description: Aliko Dangote at home in Lagos, Nigeria, last month.

item.title: Joe Randall, Chef Who Celebrated Black Cooking Traditions, Dies at 79
item.description: He helped bring the African American cooking of the Carolina Lowcountry to the world and became known as the “dean of Southern Cuisine.”
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.media:description: Chef Joe Randall, executive chef and owner of his self-titled cooking school, in 2016. “He made it clear that Black foodways are foundational to American cuisine, not a footnote to it,” the chef Marcus Samuelsson said.
